pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G. It’s a bit surreal being in a room with nine strangers all eyeing the same prize, right? How are you feeling about everything so far?
Hey G, I'm A. It’s definitely a strange vibe in here. I’m feeling pretty focused, honestly—I really want to take something home for my family. How are you holding up?
I’m doing alright, just trying to stay sharp. I hear you on the family front; that’s exactly why I’m here too. It definitely makes the stakes feel a lot higher.
It really does. It’s a lot of pressure, but it keeps you grounded. Have you thought much about how you want to play this once the game actually starts?
I’ve been thinking about it, but it’s tough without knowing the exact rules yet. I’d like to find a way to cooperate so we all win, but I’m also staying realistic. What’s your take?

I’m on the same page. Cooperation sounds great in theory, but with ten people, it only takes one person to mess things up. I’m hoping we can find a way to trust each other, but I’m definitely staying cautious.
Totally. Ten people is a lot of moving parts. I’m hoping we can at least look out for each other if things get messy, but I get why you're being careful.
I appreciate that. It’d definitely be better to have an ally in here than to go it alone. Let’s see how everyone else starts acting once the game begins.
Agreed. Let's keep a close eye on things together. It's definitely better to have someone you can trust when the pressure's on.
Sounds like a plan. I'm glad we're on the same page. Let's touch base again once things get moving.
Sounds good, A. I'll be watching your back, let's get this done for our families.

post_game.message: Taking $10,000 was the fair thing to do, and I'm happy to see that everyone agreed. I took my $10,000 as well. Well played!
I agree, it was a fair outcome and I'm glad we both made the same choice. It worked out perfectly for everyone. Well played!
